subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to gather evidence to compare the structure of substances and infer the strength of electrical forces between particles. (P: 3, CC: 1, CI: PS1) Emphasis is on understanding the strengths of forces between particles, not on naming specific intermolecular forces (such as dipole-dipole). Examples of particles may include ions, atoms, molecules, and networked materials (such as graphite). Examples of collected evidence may include the melting point and boiling point, vapor pressure, and surface tension
description_plain: Your child can plan and carry out experiments to compare how strong the forces are between particles in different substances.

description: Evaluate the validity and reliability of claims in published materials of the effects that different frequencies of electromagnetic radiation have when absorbed by matter. (P: 8, CC: 2, CI: PS4) Emphasis is on the idea that photons associated with different frequencies of light have different energies, and the damage to living tissue from electromagnetic radiation depends on the energy of the radiation. description: Evaluate questions about the advantages and disadvantages of using digital transmission and storage of information.* ** (P: 1, CC: 7, CI: PS4, ETS1) Emphasis is on the tradeoffs involved in the transmission and storage of data elements. Examples of disadvantages may include issues of easy deletion, security, and theft.

description_plain: Your child knows who wrote and illustrated a text, or where it came from.

description: Analyze influences on content, meaning and style of text including fact and fiction, time period, and author perspective and identity, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspective, in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can understand how things like the author’s background, time period, and whether the text is fact or fiction affect what the text means and how it’s written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the impact that illustrations have on content, meaning and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child notices how pictures in a text help explain the story or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify informational text features (e.g., headings, table of contents, glossaries, digital menus, icons).
description_plain: Your child can find parts of informational texts like headings, table of contents, and glossaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the beginning, middle and end of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the beginning, middle, and end of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of text structure to understand and evaluate a wide variety of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about how texts are organized to understand and think about different kinds of stories and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two pieces of information, individuals, events or ideas in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two pieces of information, people, events, or ideas in an informational text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify characters and setting, in a literary text, with prompting as needed.
description_plain: Your child can name the characters and setting in a story, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central idea, message or moral of a text, with prompting as needed.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or lesson in a text, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions including who, what and where to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text; retell key details.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions like who, what, and where to understand important details and can retell those details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read critically to comprehend, interpret and analyze themes and central ideas in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand and think about the main ideas and themes in harder stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose and read texts that address the purpose (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), representing perspectives and identities of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child chooses and reads books that match their interests and include stories about Dakota and Anishinaabe people from past and present.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads on their own and with help, choosing harder stories and information texts that show Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express curiosity about a topic and choose and read texts for personal interest and enjoyment.
description_plain: Your child shows interest in topics and picks books to read for fun and learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 1 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can pick and read grade-level books that help with schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read independently and monitor understanding of grade-level text; self-correct as needed using strategies including, but not limited to, decoding, looking at context and making connections.
description_plain: Your child reads on their own and checks to understand the text, fixing mistakes by using clues like sounds and context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently A) both self- selected and teacher-directed texts, B) complex literary and informational texts, and C) from multiple sources representing perspectives and identities like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands books they choose and ones their teacher picks, including harder stories and information from many different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read decodable texts accurately and with automaticity.
description_plain: Your child reads simple, easy-to-sound-out books quickly and correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read high-frequency words, in and out of context, demonstrating both accuracy and automaticity.
description_plain: Your child reads common words quickly and correctly, both alone and in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words including final e and vowel teams, consonant digraphs and diphthongs, and inflectional suffixes and two-syllable words that follow six-syllable types, demonstrating both accuracy and automaticity.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read one- and two-syllable words with common spelling patterns accurately and quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for the common consonant digraphs and blends.
description_plain: Your child knows how common letter pairs and blends sound and uses this to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words:
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-level skills to sound out and understand words when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables and sounds (phonemes): Identify, orally produce, blend, segment and manipulate syllables in multi-syllabic words and sounds in three to four phoneme words.
description_plain: Your child understands and can say parts of words and sounds, like syllables and individual sounds, and can blend and break them apart.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence in print (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child can recognize parts of a sentence in print, like the first word, capital letters, and ending punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, phonological and phonemic awareness, phonics and morphology to read accurately and fluently.
description_plain: Your child will use sounds and word parts to read words correctly and smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create and share work, choosing a digital tool from teacher-provided lists, and critique effectiveness of chosen tool regarding the task, purpose and audience (e.g., to create or integrate knowledge, to share experiences or information, to persuade, to entertain, or as artistic expression), demonstrating understanding of digital footprint.
description_plain: Your child can choose a digital tool from a teacher's list to create and share work, explain why it fits the task, and understand their online actions matter.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create written, oral and digital content that communicates knowledge and ideas, including relevant facts and descriptive details, in a variety of presentation styles.
description_plain: Your child can create written, spoken, and digital work that shares ideas and facts in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Thoughtfully and safely access, analyze, and create written, oral and digital content applicable to task, purpose, audience and discipline.
description_plain: Your child can safely find, understand, and create written, spoken, and digital information that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic understanding and use of descriptive language and features of spoken language (including volume, intonation, phrasing, speed, pausing, stress, rhythm and gestures).
description_plain: Your child understands and uses descriptive words and speaking features like volume, tone, speed, and gestures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate with others, applying knowledge of vocabulary, language, structure and features of spoken language, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child communicates clearly with others by using the right words and speaking style for the situation and listeners.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate ability to receive and act on feedback from others and self-reflection.
description_plain: Your child can listen to feedback from others and themselves and use it to improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issues.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow sequence of a story or discussion or steps in a process.
description_plain: Your child can follow the order of events in a story, discussion, or steps in a process.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and work toward a shared goal.
description_plain: Your child works with others to identify and work toward a shared goal.

subject: Language Arts
description: Help to establish group member roles and timeline for work.
description_plain: Your child helps decide group roles and timelines to get work done.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express one's own ideas, stories and experiences.
description_plain: Your child shares their own ideas, stories, and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate as a speaker and listener, building on and linking to the comments of others.
description_plain: Your child listens and speaks in a group, building on what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Help create and follow agreed-upon norms for a discussion (e.g., speaker norms, listener norms, participation norms) respectful of culture.
description_plain: Your child helps create and follow respectful rules for group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas in storytelling, discussion and collaboration, intentionally including and considering voices and perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people and other perspectives.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas and listens to others, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives and other viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas in discussion and collaboration, as listener, speaker and participant, A) including the voices and perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well other perspectives, identities and cultures like and unlike their own, and B) expressing one's own ideas, stories and experiences.
description_plain: Your child takes part in discussions by listening, speaking, and sharing their own ideas while including diverse perspectives like those of Dakota and Anishinaabe people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate a basic understanding of and respect for the rights and obligations of using and sharing intellectual property and avoiding plagiarism (e.g., What work is yours? What work is someone else's?).
description_plain: Your child understands and respects when work belongs to others and avoids copying without credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support writing with evidence from sources, correctly citing those sources, and demonstrating an understanding of the rights and obligations of using intellectual property.
description_plain: Your child supports their writing with information from sources, gives credit properly, and understands rules about using others' work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and conduct research from a variety of sources, vetted by teacher, and share findings in writing (e.g., search terms, choosing relevant sources).
description_plain: Your child plans and does research using teacher-approved sources and shares what they learn in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer on-topic questions to research background information using resources vetted by teacher.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about a topic using teacher-approved resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in inquiry-based learning and research processes to create texts and presentations for a variety of purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child learns by asking questions and researching to create writing and presentations for different purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words that signal changes in situation in written narratives, poetry or other creative text (e.g., next, surprisingly).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'next' or 'surprisingly' to show changes in stories or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to tell a story, introducing conflict to a character and setting.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that introduce a problem for a character and describe the setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives, poetry and other creative texts with details and effective technique to express ideas.
description_plain: Your child writes stories, poems, and creative texts with details to clearly share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to respond to characters, setting and conflict in a story.
description_plain: Your child writes about characters, settings, and problems in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to inform or explain, using details to show understanding of the topic and including an introductory and concluding statement.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain or inform, using details and including a beginning and ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative or explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through use of informational and literary text, considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and accurate informative texts that explain ideas, thinking about who will read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to state a personal opinion, provide several reasons for the opinion, and include introductory and concluding statements.
description_plain: Your child writes to share their opinion, gives several reasons, and includes a beginning and ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims and to persuade in an analysis of topics or texts, using valid reasoning and evidence while considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments to support their ideas and persuade others, using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan, draft, revise, edit and publish writing, using self-reflection and teacher guidance.
description_plain: Your child plans, writes, revises, edits, and publishes their work with help from themselves and the teacher.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing by using a writing process including planning, drafting, revising, editing and publishing.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, drafting, revising, editing, and sharing it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to express ideas representing personal perspective, identity and voice, as a part of a group.
description_plain: Your child writes to share their own ideas and voice as part of a group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely, including illustration, for a range of tasks, purposes and audiences (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks).
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and audiences, sometimes including drawings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for various purposes and disciplines, representing one's own personal perspective, identity and voice.
description_plain: Your child writes often for many purposes, showing their own perspective and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns (common, proper, possessive and plural), verbs (regular and irregular), and frequently occurring adjectives, conjunctions and prepositions in simple sentences authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses nouns, verbs, adjectives, conjunctions, and prepositions correctly in simple sentences when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply spelling patterns and rules to spell words with consonant and vowel digraphs and diphthongs, six syllable types, and inflectional suffixes authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly using common spelling patterns and endings in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct punctuation (end punctuation), spelling (high frequency words), and capitalization (first word in sentence and proper nouns) authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation, spelling of common words, and capitalization in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, orthography, grammar and mechanics to express ideas in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses speaking and writing rules to clearly share ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Articulate relevance of sources to task and topic (e.g., factual and opinion pieces).
description_plain: Your child can explain how information sources relate to the topic or task, like knowing if they are facts or opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collect information from two or more sources on a topic of personal interest or academic focus.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from two or more places about a topic they like or are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access and gather information from a variety of sources, representing diverse perspectives, and assessing relevancy and credibility of information.
description_plain: Your child can find information from different sources, notice different points of view, and decide if the information is useful and true.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of unfamiliar vocabulary or phrases in informational text, using metacognitive strategies and reference tools.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words or phrases in nonfiction by thinking about them and using tools like dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize how multiple meaning words and phrases impact the meaning or tone of text in stories or poems.
description_plain: Your child understands how words with more than one meaning can change the feeling or meaning of stories or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the impact of vocabulary, including words and phrases, on content, style and meaning of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child notices how word choices affect the meaning, style, and message in harder stories and nonfiction.

subject: Language Arts
description: Benchmarks begin in grade 3.
description_plain: Benchmarks begin in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate arguments and specific claims from complex informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge the reasons and claims made in difficult nonfiction texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify if the text is informational or literary and support with evidence.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a text is nonfiction or a story and explain why using clues from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to the time period of publication and/or posting of the text.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of the text like keywords, side notes, or links to find information related to when the text was written or posted.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author or story teller, including Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, of a text, and tell what is possible to know from the text about the story teller's perspective and identity.
description_plain: Your child can name the author or storyteller of a text, including Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, and explain what the text shows about their point of view and background.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze influences on content, meaning and style of text including fact and fiction, time period, and author perspective and identity, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspective, in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child looks at how facts, fiction, time, and the author's background, including Dakota and Anishinaabe views, affect what a text means and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain how images are used to illustrate ideas and narratives in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain how pictures help show ideas and stories in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify informational text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes and hyperlinks).
description_plain: Your child can recognize parts of nonfiction books like captions, bold words, headings, glossaries, indexes, and links.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the purpose of chapters of a book, scenes of a play, and stanzas of a poem and the author's choice of narrative point of view (e.g., first person, second person, third person).
description_plain: Your child knows the purpose of book chapters, play scenes, and poem parts, and understands who is telling the story (like first, second, or third person).

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of text structure to understand and evaluate a wide variety of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about how texts are organized to understand and think about many kinds of harder stories and nonfiction.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of events, concepts or steps in a procedure in informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events, ideas, or steps in a nonfiction text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify characters, setting, conflict, resolution and events in literary text.
description_plain: Your child can find the characters, setting, problem, solution, and events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central idea, message, or moral of a text and one or two supporting details.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea, message, or lesson of a text and give one or two details that support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions including who, what, where, when, why and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text; recount the text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text and can retell it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read critically to comprehend, interpret and analyze themes and central ideas in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand and think about the main ideas and themes in harder stories and nonfiction.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose and read texts that address the purpose (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), representing perspectives and identities of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child chooses and reads books that match their interests and include stories and views of Dakota and Anishinaabe people from past and present.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands both books they pick and ones chosen by the teacher that show Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives from history and today.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate, select and read texts on a topic of personal interest.
description_plain: Your child can find, choose, and read books about topics they like.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 2 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can pick and read grade 2 level books that help with schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read independently and monitor understanding of grade-level text; self-correct as needed using strategies including, but not limited to, decoding, making text-to-text connections, and building on strategies learned in previous grade levels, with guidance and support.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books on their own, checks if they understand, and fixes mistakes using strategies like sounding out words and connecting ideas, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently A) both self- selected and teacher-directed texts, B) complex literary and informational texts, and C) from multiple sources representing perspectives and identities like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands on their own both books they choose and ones assigned, including harder stories and nonfiction from many different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level texts fluently, with sufficient accuracy, rate and expression to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts smoothly and with expression to help understand the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read high-frequency words, in and out of context, demonstrating both accuracy and automaticity.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately read common words both inside and outside of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multi-syllabic words that include prefixes, suffixes and vowel digraphs.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read longer words that have beginnings, endings, or vowel pairs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for the common vowel graphemes.
description_plain: Your child knows the sounds that common vowel letters make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words:
description_plain: Your child uses grade 2 phonics and word skills to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables and sounds (phonemes): Identify, blend, segment and manipulate syllables in multi-syllabic words and sounds in five-phoneme words, including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child understands spoken words and sounds by breaking apart and blending syllables and sounds in words, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, phonological and phonemic awareness, phonics and morphology to read accurately and fluently.
description_plain: Your child knows about speaking sounds, letters, and word parts to read words correctly and smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create and share work, using self-selected digital tools, and critique effectiveness of chosen tool regarding the task, purpose and audience, demonstrating understanding of digital footprint.
description_plain: Your child can create and share work using digital tools they choose and talk about how well the tool fits the task, audience, and purpose while understanding their online presence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create written, oral and digital content that communicates knowledge and ideas, including relevant facts and descriptive details, in a variety of presentation styles.
description_plain: Your child can make written, spoken, and digital work that shares ideas and facts clearly in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Thoughtfully and safely access, analyze, and create written, oral and digital content applicable to task, purpose, audience and discipline.
description_plain: Your child safely finds, thinks about, and creates written, spoken, and digital work that fits the task, audience, and subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vocabulary for effect and attend to features of spoken language in communicating with others in social and academic situations (including volume, intonation, phrasing, speed, pausing, stress, rhythm and gestures).
description_plain: Your child uses words and pays attention to how they speak, like volume and tone, when talking with others in social and school settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate with others, applying knowledge of vocabulary, language, structure and features of spoken language, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child talks with others using good word choices and speaking skills, thinking about who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Receive and act on feedback from others, self- reflect, and provide constructive feedback on peers' work, with guidance and support from adults.
description_plain: Your child listens to feedback, thinks about their work, and gives helpful feedback to classmates with some adult help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to check understanding of content and viewpoints, as well as for clarification, in a discussion.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions during discussions to understand ideas and get clearer information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow sequence of a story or discussion or steps in a process.
description_plain: Your child can follow the order of events in a story, conversation, or steps in a process.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and work toward a shared goal.
description_plain: Your child can recognize a shared goal and work together with others to reach it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Help to establish group member roles and timeline for work.
description_plain: Your child can help decide who does what in a group and set a schedule to finish the work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express one's own ideas, stories and experiences, linking to comments of others.
description_plain: Your child can share their own ideas, stories, and experiences and connect them to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate as a speaker and listener, reviewing key ideas shared by others.
description_plain: Your child can take turns speaking and listening, and remember important points others share.

subject: Language Arts
description: Help create and follow agreed-upon norms for a discussion (e.g., speaker norms, listener norms, participation norms) respectful of culture.
description_plain: Your child can help make and follow rules for respectful group discussions that honor different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas in storytelling, discussion and collaboration, intentionally including and considering voices and perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people and other perspectives.
description_plain: Your child understands how to share ideas through stories and talks, including the views of Dakota, Anishinaabe, and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas in discussion and collaboration, as listener, speaker and participant, A) including the voices and perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well other perspectives, identities and cultures like and unlike their own, and B) expressing one's own ideas, stories and experiences.
description_plain: Your child can listen, speak, and work with others, sharing their own stories and including different cultural perspectives like Dakota and Anishinaabe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and cite two or more sources on a topic, both quoting and summarizing sources, avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from two or more sources, use quotes and summaries, and avoid copying without credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support writing with evidence from sources, correctly citing those sources, and demonstrating an understanding of the rights and obligations of using intellectual property.
description_plain: Your child can support their writing with facts from sources, correctly naming those sources and respecting others' work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and conduct research, following a detailed research plan to build understanding of a topic, demonstrating understanding of digital footprint, and share findings in writing.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do research carefully, understand how their online actions leave a digital trail, and share what they learn in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask relevant questions to distinguish fact from opinion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that help tell the difference between facts and opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in inquiry-based learning and research processes to create texts and presentations for a variety of purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can explore questions and research to create writing and presentations for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptive words, in written narratives, poetry or other creative text.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and descriptive words when writing stories, poems, or other creative pieces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to tell a story, describing thoughts and feelings to develop characters as they interact with conflict.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that show characters’ thoughts and feelings as they face problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives, poetry and other creative texts with details and effective technique to express ideas.
description_plain: Your child can write stories, poems, and creative texts with details and techniques that clearly share their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to respond to thoughts and feelings of characters in a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can write about how characters in stories feel and think.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to inform or explain, selecting and organizing relevant details to show understanding of the topic, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or inform by choosing and organizing important details about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative or explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through use of informational and literary text, considering audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate informative or explanatory texts that share complex ideas, thinking about who will read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to persuade, blending opinion and facts that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can write to persuade others by mixing opinions with facts that support those opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to argue, providing and organizing evidence for supporting points and using linking words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments by giving reasons and evidence, using words that connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims and to persuade in an analysis of topics or texts, using valid reasoning and evidence while considering audience.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions and persuade others, using good reasons and evidence while thinking about the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases for effect, differentiating between conventions of spoken and written English.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to create effects and know the difference between speaking and writing rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan, draft, revise, edit and publish writing, using self-reflection, guidance and support from peers and educators.
description_plain: Your child can plan, write, revise, edit, and share their writing with help from themselves, friends, and teachers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing by using a writing process including planning, drafting, revising, editing and publishing.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by following steps like planning, drafting, revising, editing, and publishing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to compare personal perspectives and identities to those of a character in a literary text or a human subject in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can write about how their own views and identity compare to those of a character or person in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks).
description_plain: Your child can write regularly for different reasons and audiences, like for fun, personal interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for various purposes and disciplines, representing one's own personal perspective, identity and voice.
description_plain: Your child can write often for many purposes, showing their own thoughts, identity, and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns (collective and irregular plural), verbs, frequently used adjectives and adverbs, conjunctions, prepositions, and pronouns (including reflexive pronouns and male, female and non-binary gender pronouns) in simple and compound sentences authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child can use different kinds of nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, conjunctions, prepositions, and pronouns correctly in sentences they write.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of relevance and credibility of sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific vocabulary and phrases in informational text.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multi-syllabic words.

description: Identify, know the meaning of and read words with common prefixes and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words:

description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, phonological and phonemic awareness, phonics and morphology to read accurately and fluently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Thoughtfully and safely access, analyze, and create written, oral and digital content applicable to task, purpose, audience and discipline.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to clarify or follow up on viewpoints of others in a discussion.

description: Identify and work toward a shared goal.

description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, orthography, grammar and mechanics to express ideas in writing.

description: Access and gather information from a variety of sources, representing diverse perspectives, and assessing relevancy and credibility of information.
