subject: Language Arts
description: Write to inform or explain, using precise, domain-specific vocabulary, developing subtopics, and grouping related information logically in chosen text structure, including using formatting (e.g., headings) and visuals when useful to aid comprehension, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain or inform using clear, specific words, organizing information well, and adding headings or pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative or explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through use of informational and literary text, considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and accurate informative texts that explain complex ideas, thinking about who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to persuade, employing emotional strategies (e.g., manipulation, motivation, inspiration), building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes to persuade others by using feelings to motivate or inspire, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to argue, situating argument in an explanation of a broad topic, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that explain a big topic clearly, using reasons and evidence, building on past skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims and to persuade in an analysis of topics or texts, using valid reasoning and evidence while considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments to support their ideas and persuade others, using good reasons and evidence while thinking about the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, punctuation and sentences to convey ideas precisely, appropriate to intended audience and context.
description_plain: Your child uses words, punctuation, and sentences carefully to share ideas clearly for the people they are writing to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and draft multiple pieces; self-select pieces to revise (e.g., rewriting or trying a new approach), edit and publish.
description_plain: Your child can plan and write several pieces, choose which ones to improve by rewriting or trying new ideas, then edit and share their final work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing by using a writing process including planning, drafting, revising, editing and publishing.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by following steps like planning, drafting, revising, editing, and publishing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to reflect how personal identity has developed over time.
description_plain: Your child can write about how they have changed and grown over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a range of tasks, purposes and audiences (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks).
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and audiences, like for fun, personal interest, or schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for various purposes and disciplines, representing one's own personal perspective, identity and voice.
description_plain: Your child writes often for many purposes, sharing their own thoughts, identity, and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs (including relative adverbs) and pronouns (including relative pronouns) in sentences authentically in writing, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child uses nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, and pronouns correctly in their writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply spelling patterns and rules to spell words with French and Latin word origin authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly by applying spelling rules, including words that come from French and Latin.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct punctuation (including semi-colons and colons), spelling, capitalization and grammar authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation like commas, colons, and semi-colons, as well as proper spelling, capitalization, and grammar in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, orthography, grammar and mechanics to express ideas in writing.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use speaking, spelling, grammar, and writing rules to clearly share their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate sources based on validity and credibility of information related to task and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can decide if information from different sources is trustworthy and useful for their task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collect information from a variety of sources in different formats, representing diverse perspectives (e.g., interviews, videos, podcasts).
description_plain: Your child gathers information from many types of sources like interviews, videos, and podcasts that show different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access and gather information from a variety of sources, representing diverse perspectives, and assessing relevancy and credibility of information.
description_plain: Your child finds and collects information from many sources with different viewpoints and checks if the information is relevant and reliable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases in academic, technical and domain-specific vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child understands special words used in school subjects or technical areas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances (e.g., connotative and denotative) in word meanings in stories, poems or songs.
description_plain: Your child understands how words can have different meanings and feelings in stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the impact of vocabulary, including words and phrases, on content, style and meaning of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child notices how word choices affect the meaning and style of difficult stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify an author's argument and how perspective and bias influence choices regarding evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find an author's main argument and see how their opinions and bias affect the evidence they use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate arguments and specific claims from complex informational texts.
description_plain: Your child evaluates arguments and specific claims in challenging informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a text's placement on a continuum of fact to fiction, citing evidence.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a text is more fact or fiction by giving examples from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare texts published in different time periods related to influences on meaning, content or style of the texts.
description_plain: Your child compares texts written in different times to see how meaning, content, or style changes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account, including those by Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child compares a firsthand and a secondhand story about the same event, including those by Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, and explains how they focus on different details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze influences on content, meaning and style of text including fact and fiction, time period, and author perspective and identity, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspective, in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how things like fact vs. fiction, time period, and the author's background, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives, affect the meaning and style of complex texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret, apply and evaluate the ideas/information conveyed through illustrations, graphics and other audiovisual elements to support understanding in text.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses pictures, graphics, and videos to help explain the ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the effects of the various informational text structures (including, but not limited to, sequence, chronology and description) on a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child recognizes how different ways of organizing information, like order or description, affect a text or part of it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes or stanzas fit together to provide the overall structure of a literary text.
description_plain: Your child explains how chapters, scenes, or stanzas work together to create the overall structure of a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of text structure to understand and evaluate a wide variety of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about how texts are organized to understand and judge many kinds of complex stories and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between individuals, events and concepts, based on specific details, in informational text.
description_plain: Your child explains how people, events, and ideas are connected in informational texts using specific details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how and when characters, setting, conflict, resolution, events or ideas are introduced and how they change or evolve in literary text.
description_plain: Your child describes how characters, settings, conflicts, and events are introduced and change in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is developed or conveyed through specific details.
description_plain: Your child finds the main theme or idea of a text and explains how details help show it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child quotes from a text to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses about its meaning, and can summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read critically to comprehend, interpret and analyze themes and central ideas in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand and think about main ideas and themes in difficult stories and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose and read texts that address the purpose (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), representing perspectives and identities of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child chooses and reads books that match their interests and show the experiences of historical and current Dakota and Anishinaabe people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands both chosen and assigned complex stories and information that include Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate, select and read texts representing various perspectives and identities like and unlike their own, using various methods of searching for text (e.g., literary award lists, curated book lists, book reviews).
description_plain: Your child finds and reads texts that show different viewpoints and identities, including those like and unlike their own, using tools like book lists and reviews.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 5 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child selects and reads grade 5 level texts well to complete school tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read independently and monitor understanding of grade-level text; self-correct as needed, independently using strategies including, but not limited to, making inferences and connecting text to background knowledge.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts on their own, checks their understanding, and fixes mistakes by using strategies like making guesses and connecting to what they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently A) both self- selected and teacher-directed texts, B) complex literary and informational texts, and C) from multiple sources representing perspectives and identities like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands both chosen and assigned complex stories and information from many sources that show different perspectives and identities, including those from dominant and marginalized groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level texts fluently with sufficient accuracy, rate and expression to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts smoothly and with expression to help understand the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words: Use knowledge of letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns and word origin (French and Latin) to decode and comprehend unfamiliar multi-syllabic words in and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of sounds, syllables, and word origins to read and understand new multi-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, phonological and phonemic awareness, phonics and morphology to read accurately and fluently.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses speaking skills, sound awareness, spelling rules, and word parts to read accurately and smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create and share, individually or in a collaborative group, an informative multi-media work or digital communication, choosing tools to meet the task, purpose and audience, demonstrating understanding of digital footprint.
description_plain: Your child creates and shares an informative multimedia or digital project alone or with others, choosing the right tools for the task and audience, while understanding how their digital actions leave a footprint.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically, including relevant facts and descriptive details to support central ideas or themes, in a variety of presentation styles, following ethical and safe communication practices.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas and findings in a clear order, using facts and details to support main points, and do this in different ways while communicating safely and respectfully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Thoughtfully and safely access, analyze, and create written, oral and digital content applicable to task, purpose, audience and discipline.
description_plain: Your child can find, understand, and create written, spoken, and digital information carefully and safely, matching the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vocabulary, language, structure and features of spoken language to communicate ideas precisely in a variety of forms, including digital and face-to-face interactions, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child can use the right words and speaking style to clearly share ideas in different ways, thinking about who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate with others, applying knowledge of vocabulary, language, structure and features of spoken language, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child can talk and share ideas with others using good vocabulary and speaking skills, while thinking about the audience and setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech, writing or communications by utilizing constructive feedback from self and others.
description_plain: Your child can improve their speaking or writing by using helpful feedback from themselves and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and respond to questions, both prepared in advance and in response to comments of others, demonstrating preparation for the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions during discussions, showing they are prepared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate, mediate and problem solve to make decisions as appropriate for productive group discussion.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to solve problems and make good decisions during group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use tools to collaborate with others synchronously and asynchronously.
description_plain: Your child can use tools like apps or websites to work together with others at the same time or at different times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas through storytelling, discussion and collaboration, intentionally considering the Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well as other perspectives like and unlike their own.
description_plain: Your child can share stories and ideas with others, including learning about Dakota and Anishinaabe people and other different viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas in discussion and collaboration, as listener, speaker and participant, A) including the voices and perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well other perspectives, identities and cultures like and unlike their own, and B) expressing one's own ideas, stories and experiences.
description_plain: Your child can join discussions by listening, speaking, and sharing their own ideas and stories, including voices from Dakota, Anishinaabe, and other cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and cite a variety of print and digital sources, paraphrasing in addition to quoting and summarizing, avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can use and give credit to different books and websites, putting information in their own words and avoiding copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support writing with evidence from sources, correctly citing those sources, and demonstrating an understanding of the rights and obligations of using intellectual property.
description_plain: Your child can support their writing with facts from sources, correctly showing where the information came from and understanding how to use others' work fairly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and conduct independent research from a variety of sources that represent varying perspectives; evaluate credibility and relevance of information, avoiding plagiarism, and share findings in writing.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do research using many sources with different views, check if the information is trustworthy and useful, avoid copying, and share what they learned in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate self-generated questions that guide inquiry and refocus inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can come up with their own questions to guide their research and change questions if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in inquiry-based learning and research processes to create texts and presentations for a variety of purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can learn by asking questions and researching to create writing and presentations for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and sensory detail to support development of literary elements, and use structure appropriate to chosen style and tone, in written narratives, poetry or other creative text.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and sensory details to make stories or poems more interesting, choosing the right style and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to create, portraying complexity in characters or self-expression in various literary forms (e.g., complex emotions, motivations, experiences and characters who change over time).
description_plain: Your child can write stories or poems that show deep feelings, reasons, and changes in characters or themselves.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives, poetry and other creative texts with details and effective technique to express ideas.
description_plain: Your child can write creative stories, poems, or other texts with details and good techniques to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to respond to a literary text, demonstrating understanding of character complexity (e.g., writing personal reactions, analysis, and interpretation of text).
description_plain: Your child can write about a story they read, showing they understand the characters’ feelings and actions by sharing their thoughts and analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to inform or explain, introducing diverse perspectives, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives, about a topic, using precise, domain-specific vocabulary, and utilize organization strategies common in a variety of text structures, building on skills from previous years (e.g., description, classification, cause/effect, comparison/contrast, problem/solution, proposition/support, critique, inductive/reductive).
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic, including different views like those of Dakota and Anishinaabe people, using clear and specific words and organized ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative or explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through use of informational and literary text, considering audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate informative texts that explain complex ideas, thinking about who will read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to persuade, employing emotional strategies (e.g., manipulation, motivation, inspiration) and articulating benefits of the strategies used, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child can write to persuade others by using emotions and explaining why their ideas are helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to argue, articulating both an argument and a counter-argument, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child can write to argue a point by sharing both their opinion and the opposite view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims and to persuade in an analysis of topics or texts, using valid reasoning and evidence while considering audience.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments to support their ideas and convince others using good reasons and evidence, thinking about who they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary word choice, showing understanding of denotation and connotation, phrases, and sentence structures as applicable to context, to convey ideas precisely and engage intended audience.
description_plain: Your child can choose words and sentence styles carefully to clearly express ideas and keep the reader interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and draft multiple pieces; self-select pieces to take through the writing process and methods for revision; edit and publish.
description_plain: Your child can plan and write several pieces, choose which to improve, and then revise, edit, and share their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing by using a writing process including planning, drafting, revising, editing and publishing.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, drafting, revising, editing, and publishing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to reflect how personal perspective, identity and voice have developed over time.
description_plain: Your child can write about how their own views, identity, and voice have changed over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a range of tasks (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), purposes and audiences, choosing topics and format.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly for different reasons and audiences, picking topics and formats they like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for various purposes and disciplines, representing one's own personal perspective, identity and voice.
description_plain: Your child can write often for many purposes, showing their own thoughts, identity, and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns, verbs (correctly using verb tense), adjectives, adverbs and pronouns in sentences authentically in writing, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child can use nouns, verbs with correct tenses, adjectives, adverbs, and pronouns correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply spelling patterns and rules to spell words with Greek word origin authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child can spell words with Greek origins correctly by applying spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct punctuation (including dashes, hyphens and ellipses), spelling, capitalization and grammar authentically in writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct punctuation, spelling, capitalization, and grammar in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, orthography, grammar and mechanics to express ideas in writing.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use speaking, spelling, grammar, and writing rules to express ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate perspective, credibility and relevancy of sources related to task and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can judge if information sources are trustworthy, relevant, and fit the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access and make choices about information from a variety of sources, representing diverse perspectives, articulating how understanding is impacted by format/mode of information.
description_plain: Your child can find and choose information from many sources with different views and explain how the format affects understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access and gather information from a variety of sources, representing diverse perspectives, and assessing relevancy and credibility of information.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources with different perspectives and decide if the information is useful and believable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine academic and technical vocabulary in informational text through study of word origins (morphology and etymology) and use of reference tools.
description_plain: Your child can study difficult words in informational texts by learning their origins and using reference tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of a specific word choice, and rhythm or meter, on the meaning and tone of a story, poem or song (word choice analysis including, but not limited to, vocabulary, assonance, consonance and rhyme).
description_plain: Your child can explain how specific word choices and rhythm affect the meaning and feeling of stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the impact of vocabulary, including words and phrases, on content, style and meaning of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can look at how words and phrases affect the meaning, style, and message of complex stories and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between relevant and irrelevant evidence to evaluate an author's argument.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between important and unimportant evidence when looking at an author's argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate arguments and specific claims from complex informational texts.
description_plain: Your child will evaluate arguments and specific claims in difficult informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the specific central theme or argument in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by facts and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the main theme or argument in a text, knowing which claims have facts to support them and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine similar texts published in different time periods, comparing and contrasting influence on meaning, content or style of the texts.
description_plain: Your child compares texts from different time periods to see how time affects their meaning, content, or style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts by various cultures, including Dakota and Anishinaabe, of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child looks at stories about the same event from different cultures, like Dakota and Anishinaabe, and notices important similarities and differences in their viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze influences on content, meaning and style of text including fact and fiction, time period, and author perspective and identity, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspective, in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child studies how things like fact or fiction, time period, and the author's background, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives, affect the meaning and style of complex texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret, apply and evaluate the ideas/information conveyed through illustrations, graphics and other audiovisual elements to support understanding and compare and contrast illustrations, graphics and other audiovisual elements in a wide variety of texts.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses pictures, graphics, and videos to help understand and compare different texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a given sentence, paragraph or section fits into the overall structure of an informational text (including, but not limited to, description, classification, cause/effect and comparison/contrast) and contributes to the development of ideas.
description_plain: Your child explains how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole informational text and helps develop its ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a given sentence, chapter, scene or stanza fits into the overall structure, including narrative point of view, of a literature text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting or plot.
description_plain: Your child explains how parts like sentences, chapters, or scenes fit into the story’s structure and help develop the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of text structure to understand and evaluate a wide variety of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of how texts are organized to understand and judge many kinds of complex stories and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a key individual, event or concept is introduced, illustrated and elaborated in informational text.
description_plain: Your child explains how an important person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular text's plot unfolds in episodes and how the characters respond in relation to the plot advancement in literary text.
description_plain: Your child describes how a story’s plot happens in parts and how characters react as the story moves forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between stated and inferred central ideas; determine the themes and central ideas and support determination with evidence and inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between main ideas that are clearly stated and those that are implied, and supports these ideas with evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what a text says explicitly and inferences drawn from the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child finds and uses evidence from the text to explain what it says clearly and what can be understood by reading between the lines, and can summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read critically to comprehend, interpret and analyze themes and central ideas in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand and explain the main ideas and themes in difficult stories and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose and read texts that address the purpose (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), representing perspectives and identities of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child chooses and reads books that match their interests and schoolwork and include perspectives of historical and modern Dakota and Anishinaabe people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands both chosen and assigned complex stories and informational texts that show Dakota and Anishinaabe viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate, select and read texts representing various perspectives and identities from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups, using various methods of searching for text (e.g., literary award lists, curated book lists, book reviews).
description_plain: Your child finds and reads texts showing different viewpoints from majority, minority, and less heard social groups using different ways to search for books.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 6 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child picks and reads grade 6 level texts that help with school tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read independently and self-monitor understanding of grade-level text; annotate learning (i.e., underline, highlight, and take notes), with guidance and support, independently applying strategies when meaning breaks down, including, but not limited to, connecting to prior knowledge, using context clues, reading ahead and looking back.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts on their own, checks their understanding, marks important parts, and uses strategies like connecting to what they know or using context clues when they don’t understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently A) both self- selected and teacher-directed texts, B) complex literary and informational texts, and C) from multiple sources representing perspectives and identities like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands both chosen and assigned complex stories and informational texts from many viewpoints, including those like and unlike their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words: Use knowledge of letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns and word origin (Greek) to decode and comprehend unfamiliar multi-syllabic words in and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word origins to read and understand new multi-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, phonological and phonemic awareness, phonics and morphology to read accurately and fluently.
description_plain: Your child shows they can read accurately and smoothly by using skills about sounds, word parts, and how words work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create and share, individually or in a collaborative group, an artistic or entertaining multi-media work or digital communication, choosing tools to meet the task, purpose and audience, demonstrating understanding of digital footprint.
description_plain: Your child creates and shares artistic or fun multimedia projects alone or with others, choosing the right tools and understanding how their digital actions leave a trace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically, including relevant facts and descriptive details to support central ideas or themes, in a variety of presentation styles, following ethical and safe communication practices; collaborate with peers, such that multiple aspects of a topic are explored (e.g. collaborative magazine, blog or presentation).
description_plain: Your child shares ideas and findings clearly and in order, using facts and details to support main points, in different presentation styles, while working safely and respectfully with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Media Literacy in Exchanging Ideas: Thoughtfully and safely access, analyze, and create written, oral and digital content applicable to task, purpose, audience and discipline.
description_plain: Your child thoughtfully and safely finds, understands, and creates written, spoken, and digital content that fits the task, purpose, audience, and subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vocabulary, language, structure and features of spoken language to establish personal voice, style and tone in communicating with others, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child uses words, language, and speaking style to show their personal voice and tone when talking to others, thinking about who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate with others, applying knowledge of vocabulary, language, structure and features of spoken language, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child communicates with others using good vocabulary and speaking skills, keeping in mind the audience and situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech, writing or communications by utilizing constructive feedback from self and others.
description_plain: Your child improves their speaking or writing by using helpful feedback from themselves and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions that elicit elaboration and respond to questions with relevant observations and ideas, demonstrating preparation for the discussion.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that encourage more detailed answers and responds with relevant ideas, showing they are ready for discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate, mediate and problem solve to make decisions as appropriate for productive group discussion.
description_plain: Your child works with others to solve problems and make good decisions during group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use tools to collaborate with others both synchronously and asynchronously.
description_plain: Your child uses tools to work together with others both at the same time and at different times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge and elaborate on others' ideas.
description_plain: Your child listens to others’ ideas and adds their own thoughts to keep the conversation going.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas through storytelling, discussion and collaboration, intentionally considering the perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well as other perspectives.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas through stories, talks, and teamwork, thoughtfully including perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people and others.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and conduct independent research, synthesizing information from a variety of sources, and share findings in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate self-generated questions that narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
