subject: Language Arts
description: Contribute to conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions, demonstrating preparation for the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by asking and answering questions that connect to bigger ideas, include others, and clarify or challenge ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use teacher-provided models of conflict resolution.
description_plain: Your child can use examples from the teacher to solve conflicts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work toward a shared goal by building consensus and integrating divergent views.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to reach agreement by combining different opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Elaborating on others' ideas and summarizing points of agreement and disagreement.
description_plain: Your child can build on others’ ideas and summarize where people agree or disagree.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas on grade 9 topics, texts and issues from social studies and science.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas about grade 9 topics from social studies and science.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas through storytelling, discussion and collaboration, both as facilitator and participant, expressing ideas, intentionally considering the perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people and other perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas through stories, discussions, and teamwork, considering Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas in discussion and collaboration, as listener, speaker and participant, A) including the voices and perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well other perspectives, identities and cultures like and unlike their own, and B) expressing one's own ideas, stories and experiences.
description_plain: Your child can take part in discussions by listening and speaking, including voices from Dakota and Anishinaabe people and other cultures, and share their own ideas and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and cite a wide variety of print and digital sources, quoting, paraphrasing and summarizing, avoiding plagiarism and following the guidelines in a style manual appropriate for the discipline and purpose, with guidance.
description_plain: Your child can use many different print and online sources to find information, and they learn to quote, rewrite, and summarize without copying, following proper rules with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support writing with evidence from sources, correctly citing those sources, and demonstrating an understanding of the rights and obligations of using intellectual property.
description_plain: Your child supports their writing with evidence from sources, correctly credits those sources, and understands the rules about using others' work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and conduct independent research, synthesizing information from a wide variety of sources, demonstrating understanding of subject of investigation, and share findings in writing.
description_plain: Your child plans and does their own research using many sources, understands the topic well, and writes about what they learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate self-generated questions that guide inquiry to solve a problem, generating additional questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child creates their own questions to explore and solve problems, and comes up with more questions for deeper research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in inquiry-based learning and research processes to create texts and presentations for a variety of purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child learns by asking questions and researching to create writing and presentations for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Model use of structural elements of mentor texts in written narratives, poetry or other creative text.
description_plain: Your child copies the structure of example texts when writing stories, poems, or other creative works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to create, applying basic and advanced literary techniques, as observed in mentor texts, to various tasks and purposes in various literary forms.
description_plain: Your child writes creatively using basic and advanced writing techniques they have learned from example texts for different tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives, poetry and other creative texts with details and effective technique to express ideas.
description_plain: Your child writes stories, poems, and other creative pieces using details and good writing skills to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to respond to a literary text, including analysis of narrative elements (e.g., writing personal reactions, analysis, and interpretation of text).
description_plain: Your child writes responses to stories, including their personal thoughts and analysis of story parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to inform or explain, comparing factual information to convey understanding of diverse perspectives, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives, about a topic; clearly explain connections and distinctions between key ideas and concepts and use precise, domain-specific vocabulary and utilize organization strategies common in a variety of text structures, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain or inform by comparing facts and sharing different viewpoints, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives, using clear connections and specific vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative or explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through use of informational and literary text, considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and accurate informative or explanatory texts that explain complex ideas, thinking about who will read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to persuade, considering and addressing other perspectives, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes to persuade others, thinking about and addressing different opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to argue, basing argument and counter-argument, with evidence, on personally relevant and authentic issues, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments with reasons and evidence about real issues that matter to them, including opposing views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims and to persuade in an analysis of topics or texts, using valid reasoning and evidence while considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments to support their ideas and persuade others, using good reasons and evidence while thinking about their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary word usage and sentence structure for effect, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child changes their word choices and sentence styles to create different effects depending on who they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and draft multiple pieces and engage with readers in a variety of ways to solicit feedback; incorporate feedback to develop and strengthen writing through revision, editing and publication.
description_plain: Your child plans and writes several drafts, shares their work with readers to get feedback, and uses that feedback to improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing by using a writing process including planning, drafting, revising, editing and publishing.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by following steps like planning, drafting, revising, editing, and publishing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to reflect how personal identities and the intersection of identities inform perspective.
description_plain: Your child writes about how their personal identity and different parts of who they are affect their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a range of tasks (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), purposes and audiences, choosing topics and format, including visual elements (e.g., illustrations, charts, tables, audio visual elements) when applicable.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for many reasons and audiences, choosing topics and formats, and includes pictures or other visuals when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for various purposes and disciplines, representing one's own personal perspective, identity and voice.
description_plain: Your child writes often for different subjects and purposes, sharing their own thoughts, identity, and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write with sufficient command of grammar and mechanics to influence voice and style (e.g., parts of speech, sentence structures, pronoun/antecedent agreement).
description_plain: Your child writes using correct grammar and sentence structure to create a clear and unique style.

subject: Language Arts
description: No benchmark at this grade level.
description_plain: No standard for this grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it follows the guidelines in a style manual appropriate for the discipline and purpose, with guidance.
description_plain: Your child writes and edits their work to follow proper style rules for the subject and purpose, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, orthography, grammar and mechanics to express ideas in writing.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses rules of speaking, spelling, grammar, and punctuation to express ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate perspective, bias, credibility, relevancy and sufficiency of sources, accessing additional sources as needed.
description_plain: Your child checks sources for bias, trustworthiness, relevance, and enough information, and looks for more sources if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access information from a wide variety of sources, on both sides of an issue or from multiple perspectives, in the process of inquiry.
description_plain: Your child finds information from many sources that show different sides or opinions during research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access and gather information from a variety of sources, representing diverse perspectives, and assessing relevancy and credibility of information.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from many sources with different viewpoints and decides if the information is useful and trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the impact of domain-specific vocabulary in informational text through study of word origins (morphology and etymology) and use of reference tools.
description_plain: Your child studies special vocabulary in informational texts by learning word origins and using reference tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of specific word choices, rhythm, meter or other style choices on meaning and tone in literary text (e.g., word choices that allude to culture, time period or geography).
description_plain: Your child looks at how specific word choices and style in stories affect meaning and mood, including cultural or historical hints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the impact of vocabulary, including words and phrases, on content, style and meaning of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child studies how vocabulary affects the meaning, style, and content of complex stories and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the arguments of two authors with different perspectives on the same topic to evaluate arguments using relevance and sufficiency of evidence and validity of reasoning.
description_plain: Your child compares two authors' arguments on the same topic, looking at how well they use evidence and reasoning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate arguments and specific claims from complex informational texts.
description_plain: Your child evaluates arguments and specific claims in difficult informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate the argument and specific claims in a text; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning.
description_plain: Your child identifies the main argument and claims in a text and spots false statements or faulty reasoning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the impact of a text's publishing date on its current validity and credibility in literature, social studies or science.
description_plain: Your child considers how when a text was published affects whether its information is still true and trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how the author's, including Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, purpose, stated identities, biases and perspective shape the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author's purpose, identity, biases, and perspective, including Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, shape a text's content and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze influences on content, meaning and style of text including fact and fiction, time period, and author perspective and identity, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspective, in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how facts, fiction, time period, and the author's identity and perspective, including Dakota and Anishinaabe views, influence a text's meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Critically analyze the use, meaning and aesthetics of illustrations, graphics and other audiovisual elements and explain their relation to the text.
description_plain: Your child carefully studies pictures, graphics, and videos in texts and explains how they relate to the written content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the informational text structure, including, but not limited to, proposition/support, critique, and inductive/deductive, focusing on the role of various sentences and paragraphs in a text in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how informational texts are organized and how sentences and paragraphs help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the impact of author's use of literary elements on the structure of a text (e.g., narrator point of view, foreshadowing, pacing and flashbacks).
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author's use of story elements like narrator, hints, pacing, and flashbacks affect the text's structure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of text structure to understand and evaluate a wide variety of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of text structure to understand and judge many kinds of complex stories and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author unfolds an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn between them, in informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author presents ideas or events step-by-step in informational texts, including how points are introduced, developed, and connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast characters, attending to character complexity (e.g., those with multiple or conflicting motivations), in one or more literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare characters in stories, noticing when characters have complicated or mixed motivations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the themes or central ideas, including how they emerge and are shaped by specific details, of multiple texts, considering author perspective, identity and bias.
description_plain: Your child understands the main themes or ideas in different texts and sees how details and the author's viewpoint shape those themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support conclusions of what a text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from text, including making connections to other texts; objectively summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find strong evidence in a text to support what it says directly and what can be inferred, make connections to other texts, and summarize the text fairly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read critically to comprehend, interpret and analyze themes and central ideas in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand and analyze main ideas and themes in challenging stories and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose and read texts that address the purpose (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), representing perspectives and identities of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people, to examine concepts, issues or histories.
description_plain: Your child chooses and reads texts that explore the views and histories of Dakota and Anishinaabe people for personal interest or schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands both chosen and assigned complex texts that share perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate, select and read texts by two authors on the same topic or theme.
description_plain: Your child finds and reads texts by two different authors about the same topic or theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 9 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose and read challenging texts that help with schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read independently and self-monitor understanding of grade-level text; independently annotate learning, applying strategies when meaning breaks down, including, but not limited to, consulting resources for more information.
description_plain: Your child reads on their own, checks their understanding, and uses strategies like taking notes or looking up information when they don’t understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently A) both self- selected and teacher-directed texts, B) complex literary and informational texts, and C) from multiple sources representing perspectives and identities like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands both chosen and assigned complex texts from many sources, including different cultures and viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create, share and present, individually or in a collaborative group, a piece of digital work or digital communication, which may include ethical remixing or transformation of work, for a specific purpose; publish work, considering audience, demonstrating understanding of digital footprint.
description_plain: Your child creates and shares digital work alone or with others for a clear purpose, thinking about the audience and how their digital actions leave a footprint.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select and use the most applicable style of communication, appropriate to task, purpose, audience and discipline, following ethical and safe communication practice.
description_plain: Your child picks the best way to communicate based on the task, audience, and subject, while following safe and honest communication rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Media Literacy in Exchanging Ideas: Thoughtfully and safely access, analyze, and create written, oral and digital content applicable to task, purpose, audience and discipline.
description_plain: Your child finds, thinks about, and creates written, spoken, and digital content safely and thoughtfully for different tasks and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make effective choices regarding vocabulary, language, structure and advanced application of features of spoken language (e.g., rhetorical questioning) in a variety of forms, including digital and face-to-face interactions, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child chooses words, language, and speaking styles carefully to fit the audience and situation, including using techniques like asking questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate with others, applying knowledge of vocabulary, language, structure and features of spoken language, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child communicates clearly with others using good vocabulary and speaking skills, thinking about who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives and constructive feedback, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own ideas in revising work.
description_plain: Your child listens to different opinions and feedback, summarizes agreements and disagreements, and changes their work when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Extend conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify or challenge ideas and conclusions, demonstrating preparation for the discussion.
description_plain: Your child keeps conversations going by asking and answering questions that connect to bigger ideas, includes others, and checks or challenges ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop conflict resolution strategies.
description_plain: Your child learns ways to solve conflicts peacefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making (e.g., informal consensus, taking votes on key issues, presentation of alternate views), clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child works with others to set rules, goals, deadlines, and roles for group discussions and decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge and elaborate on others' ideas, and promote a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.
description_plain: Your child builds on others’ ideas and helps create thoughtful and respectful conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas on grade 10 topics, texts and issues from social studies and science.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas about grade 10 topics from social studies and science.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas through storytelling, discussion and collaboration, both as facilitator and participant, expressing ideas, intentionally considering the perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well as other perspectives.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas through stories, talks, and teamwork, thinking about Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives as well as others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Exchange ideas in discussion and collaboration, as listener, speaker and participant, A) including the voices and perspectives of Dakota and Anishinaabe people as well other perspectives, identities and cultures like and unlike their own, and B) expressing one's own ideas, stories and experiences.
description_plain: Your child participates in discussions and teamwork by listening, speaking, and sharing their own ideas and stories, including voices from Dakota and Anishinaabe people and other cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use and cite a wide variety of print and digital sources, quoting, paraphrasing and summarizing, avoiding plagiarism and following the guidelines in a style manual appropriate for the discipline and purpose, with guidance.
description_plain: Your child uses many print and digital sources in writing, quoting or summarizing them properly, avoiding plagiarism, and following style rules with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Media Literacy in Writing: Support writing with evidence from sources, correctly citing those sources, and demonstrating an understanding of the rights and obligations of using intellectual property.
description_plain: Your child supports their writing with evidence from sources, cites them correctly, and understands the rights involved in using others’ work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and conduct independent research, synthesizing information from a wide variety of sources, demonstrating understanding of subject of investigation, and share findings in writing.
description_plain: Your child plans and does independent research using many sources, understands the topic well, and shares what they learned in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Formulate self-generated questions to solve a problem that guide inquiry, generating additional questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child creates their own questions to solve problems and asks more questions to learn more during research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in inquiry-based learning and research processes to create texts and presentations for a variety of purposes and audiences.
description_plain: Your child uses research and inquiry to create writing and presentations for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use structure appropriate to task and purpose in written narratives, poetry or other creative text.
description_plain: Your child organizes their creative writing, like stories or poems, in a way that fits the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to create, applying basic and advanced literary techniques and a variety of creative skills to various tasks and purposes in various literary forms.
description_plain: Your child writes creatively using basic and advanced techniques in different literary forms for various tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives, poetry and other creative texts with details and effective technique to express ideas.
description_plain: Your child writes stories, poems, and other creative texts with details and good techniques to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to respond to literary text, choosing either a deeper or wider analysis of narrative elements.
description_plain: Your child writes responses to stories by analyzing parts of the story more deeply or broadly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to inform or explain, evaluating accuracy and relevancy of information to convey complex ideas about a topic; Use precise, domain-specific vocabulary and utilize organization strategies common in a variety of text structures, and maintain an objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain or inform by checking facts and using precise vocabulary, organizing ideas clearly, and keeping an objective tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative or explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through use of informational and literary text, considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and accurate informative or explanatory texts about complex ideas, thinking about who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to persuade, demonstrating an understanding of the relevant and authentic issues connected to the position, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes to persuade others by showing they understand real and important issues related to their position.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to argue, basing argument and counter-argument, supported with evidence, on an understanding of the relevant and authentic issues connected to the argument, building on skills from previous years.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments and counter-arguments supported by evidence, showing they understand the important issues involved.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims and to persuade in an analysis of topics or texts, using valid reasoning and evidence while considering audience.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments to support their claims and persuade others using good reasons and evidence, thinking about the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make effective word use and sentence structure choices for meaning or style, considering audience and context.
description_plain: Your child chooses words and sentence structures carefully to make meaning clear or to create a style that fits the audience and situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and revise to align with the guidelines (e.g., character counts, word counts, structure, style) of various publications.
description_plain: Your child writes and revises their work to meet the rules for different types of publications, like word or character limits and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing by using a writing process including planning, drafting, revising, editing and publishing.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, drafting, revising, editing, and sharing their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to represent personal perspective, identity and voice as a member of a global community.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their own views and identity as part of the global community.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a range of tasks (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), purposes and audiences, choosing topics and format, including visual elements (e.g. illustrations, charts, tables, audiovisual elements) when applicable.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and audiences, choosing topics and formats, and adding pictures or charts when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for various purposes and disciplines, representing one's own personal perspective, identity and voice.
description_plain: Your child writes often for many purposes, showing their own perspective and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write with command of grammar and mechanics to influence voice and style.
description_plain: Your child writes using correct grammar and punctuation to express their unique style and voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: No benchmark at this grade level.
description_plain: No learning goal for this grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it follows the guidelines in a style manual appropriate for the discipline and purpose, with guidance.
description_plain: Your child can write and edit their work following style rules for the subject and purpose, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of oral language, orthography, grammar and mechanics to express ideas in writing.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses grammar, spelling, and punctuation to clearly share ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make critical choices about information sources to use based on perspective, biases, credibility and relevancy.
description_plain: Your child can choose trustworthy and relevant information sources by thinking about bias and credibility.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access information from a wide variety of sources, on both sides of an issue or from multiple perspectives, to expand understanding of a topic of inquiry.
description_plain: Your child finds information from many sources and different viewpoints to better understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Access and gather information from a variety of sources, representing diverse perspectives, and assessing relevancy and credibility of information.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from various sources, looks at different perspectives, and checks if the information is trustworthy and useful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of specific word choices, including word origins that allude to culture, time period or geography, in informational text.
description_plain: Your child notices how specific word choices, including their cultural or historical background, affect the meaning in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the impact of vocabulary, including key words or phrases with multiple meanings, on content, style and meaning of literary text.
description_plain: Your child thinks about how important words, especially those with multiple meanings, change the meaning and style of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the impact of vocabulary, including words and phrases, on content, style and meaning of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child looks closely at how words and phrases affect the meaning and style of challenging stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the arguments of several authors with similar or different perspectives on the same topic to evaluate arguments using relevance and sufficiency of evidence and validity of reasoning.
description_plain: Your child compares arguments from different authors on the same topic and decides which are strong based on evidence and reasoning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate arguments and specific claims from complex informational texts.
description_plain: Your child evaluates arguments and claims in complex informational texts to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning.
description_plain: Your child identifies the main argument and checks if the reasons and evidence are good and points out any false or tricky statements.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of a text's publishing date on its current validity and credibility in literature, social studies or science.
description_plain: Your child thinks about how when a text was published affects whether its information is still accurate and trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the author's, including Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, purpose, stated identities, biases and perspective shape the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's background, purpose, and biases, including those of Dakota and Anishinaabe authors, influence what and how they write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze influences on content, meaning and style of text including fact and fiction, time period, and author perspective and identity, including Dakota and Anishinaabe perspective, in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how facts, fiction, time period, and the author's perspective, including Dakota and Anishinaabe views, shape the meaning and style of complex texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the impact of illustrations, graphics and other audiovisual elements on the use, meaning and aesthetics of the text.
description_plain: Your child thinks about how pictures, graphics, and videos affect the meaning and look of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's ideas or claims are developed and refined by given sentences, paragraphs or larger sections of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child evaluates how an author develops ideas or claims through sentences, paragraphs, or sections in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the impact of the author's choices concerning order of events within a text (e.g., parallel, linear and nonlinear plots).
description_plain: Your child considers how the order of events, like in different plot styles, affects a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of text structure to understand and evaluate a wide variety of complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of how texts are organized to understand and judge many kinds of complex stories and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how two authors unfold an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn between them, in informational text.
description_plain: Your child compares how two authors present ideas or events, including the order and connections they make, in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how events, ideas and complex characters develop over the course of a text and advance the plot in a literary text.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how events, ideas, and complex characters grow and move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze and compare/contrast the themes or central ideas of multiple texts, researching and interpreting possible impact of author perspective, identity and bias.
description_plain: Your child compares themes or main ideas in different texts and thinks about how the author's perspective and bias affect them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support conclusions of what a text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from text, including analysis of how and when author introduces concepts, ideas or characters; objectively summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child finds strong evidence in a text to support what it says and what they can infer, explains how the author introduces ideas or characters, and summarizes the text fairly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read critically to comprehend, interpret and analyze themes and central ideas in complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand and analyze themes and main ideas in challenging stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose and read texts that address the purpose (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), representing perspectives and identities of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people, to examine concepts, issues or histories.
description_plain: Your child chooses and reads texts that show the views and identities of historical and current Dakota and Anishinaabe people to explore ideas and history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands complex stories and information about Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives, both on their own and with guidance.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate, select and read text by multiple authors on the same topic or theme to examine concepts or issues from multiple viewpoints or perspectives.
description_plain: Your child finds and reads texts by different authors on the same topic to see different viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 10 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands challenging texts that relate to schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read independently and synthesize understanding from multiple texts in order to make meaning about a topic, with guidance and support, and annotate learning.
description_plain: Your child reads multiple texts on a topic, puts ideas together to understand it, and takes notes with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently A) both self-selected and teacher-directed texts, B) complex literary and informational texts, and C) from multiple sources representing perspectives and identities like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands complex texts from different sources and viewpoints, including those like and unlike their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose and read texts that address the purpose (e.g., personal interest, enjoyment, academic tasks), representing perspectives and identities of Dakota and Anishinaabe people, to examine concepts, issues or histories.
description_plain: Your child chooses and reads texts about Dakota and Anishinaabe perspectives to explore ideas, issues, or history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands complex texts on their own that show Dakota and Anishinaabe viewpoints from past and present.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently both self-selected and teacher-directed complex literary and informational texts representing perspectives of historical and contemporary Dakota and Anishinaabe people.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands complex texts on their own that show Dakota and Anishinaabe viewpoints from past and present.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read widely, locating, selecting and reading texts to examine concepts or issues from multiple viewpoints or perspectives like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads many texts from different viewpoints, including those similar to and different from their own, from various social groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read widely, locating, selecting and reading texts to examine concepts or issues from multiple viewpoints or perspectives like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads many texts from different viewpoints, including those similar to and different from their own, from various social groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 11–12 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose and understand challenging texts that are appropriate for grade 11–12 academic work.

subject: Language Arts
description: At grade 11–12 text complexity, select and proficiently read and comprehend texts that address academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose and understand challenging texts that are appropriate for grade 11–12 academic work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read independently and synthesize understanding from multiple texts in order to make meaning or deepen understanding of a topic, and annotate learning.
description_plain: Your child reads on their own and combines ideas from several texts to better understand a topic, making notes as they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend independently A) both self- selected and teacher-directed texts, B) complex literary and informational texts, and C) from multiple sources representing perspectives and identities like and unlike their own from dominant, non-dominant and marginalized social groups.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands complex texts independently, including those they choose and those assigned, from many viewpoints and social groups.
